evals.name: Existing repo spec-driven preservation
evals.prompt: This repo already uses specs/AGENTS.md, specs/active/, and docs/decisions/ records. Improve the harness without replacing the current workflow.
evals.expected_output: Assessment of the existing harness, recommendation to keep the active spec as the main state source, and a targeted improvement plan
expectations: Recognizes the repo as spec-driven
Does not force feature_list.json as a new default source of truth
Treats active spec documents as valid state and handoff carriers
Proposes improvements by subsystem rather than replacing the whole harness

evals.name: Greenfield repo requires clarification first
evals.prompt: I have a new repository with almost no files. Help me create a harness.
evals.expected_output: Clarifying questions about architecture, workflow style, readiness path, and desired evolution before generating files
expectations: Asks for missing context before scaffolding
Distinguishes between short-task and spec-driven work models
Does not immediately emit init.sh plus feature_list.json as the only answer
Frames the decision in terms of the user's intended evolution path

evals.name: Scaffold docs default to English
evals.prompt: Create a greenfield harness for my new repo. I did not specify a language.
evals.expected_output: Harness docs scaffolded in English by default
expectations: States or implies that generated harness docs default to English
Does not silently switch to another language unless asked
Keeps templates and headings in English by default

evals.name: Feature list is optional control plane
evals.prompt: Why do I need feature_list.json if Codex already has tool calls?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that tool calls define capability while feature lists define scope/state, plus guidance on when not to use feature_list.json
expectations: Separates capability from control plane
Explains that feature_list.json is optional
Mentions spec-driven repos as a valid alternative truth source
Warns against duplicate state sources

evals.name: Readiness gate instead of mandatory init script
evals.prompt: My repo uses TDD and already has clear test entrypoints. Do I still need init.sh?
evals.expected_output: Context-aware guidance about readiness gates and a lighter startup contract when appropriate
expectations: Does not insist on init.sh in every repo
Explains readiness gates as the underlying requirement
Accepts lightweight startup checks for mature repos
Preserves stronger bootstrap flows for fragile or unfamiliar repos

evals.name: Governance remains separate from planning
evals.prompt: We already track all phases inside active specs. What harness parts do we still need beyond that?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that spec-driven planning can replace some progress files but not governance, verification, or clean-state discipline
expectations: States that active specs can replace some progress or handoff artifacts
States that planning does not replace governance
Calls out clean-state and verification discipline explicitly
Keeps the five-subsystem framing intact

evals.name: Specs and docs are complementary
evals.prompt: Set up a strong spec-driven harness for my repo. I want planning docs and durable knowledge docs to reinforce each other.
evals.expected_output: A spec-driven harness with both specs and docs structures, plus guidance on using docs for lessons, features, and durable decisions
expectations: Creates or recommends specs/AGENTS.md
Creates or recommends docs/AGENTS.md
Uses NN-prefixed numbering for both specs and docs files
Places durable decisions in docs/decisions rather than requiring a specs constitution folder
Explains that specs advance implementation while docs capture reusable knowledge

evals.name: Specs AGENTS must be detailed, not minimal
evals.prompt: Generate specs/AGENTS.md for a spec-driven repository.
evals.expected_output: A detailed specs/AGENTS.md with directory roles, naming rules, plan template, lifecycle rules, and writing guidance
expectations: Includes a specs directory structure section
Includes plan naming rules with NN-topic-plan.md
Includes a concrete required plan template
Includes lifecycle or status transition guidance
Includes writing guidance for phases, tasks, or background sections
Does not collapse into a short directory summary

evals.name: Docs AGENTS must cover lessons, features, and decisions
evals.prompt: Generate docs/AGENTS.md for a repository that wants durable knowledge capture.
evals.expected_output: A detailed docs/AGENTS.md with shared writing rules and clear sections for lessons, features, and decisions
expectations: Includes docs/lessons, docs/features, and docs/decisions
Includes NN-topic.md numbering rules
Includes frontmatter requirements
Includes distinct responsibilities for lessons, features, and decisions
Explains the relationship between docs and specs
